transcript: seemoos reyan rom 302 okay he is very very agitated tonight again again same like last week and the week before also he keep pulling his catheter and we already replace it twice this shift alone he pull it out first time around 8pm and we reinsert it and he pull it again 11pm and we reinsert it again and now 3am he is trying to pull again we put mittens on and doctar aware and family called son michael he say okay do whatever is necessary and seemoos is shouting very loud disturbing other resident in corridor and we give lorazepam 0.5 oral at 9pm no effect and then we give haloperidol 1mg intramucular at 11.30pm and he settle little bit but then agitate again and his bp is 190 over 115 very high doctar review and say we may need to transfer if not settling and his sats are 94 on room air and he has chest infection also on day four of co amoxiclav and temp is 38.2 and rr 22 and we increase oxygen to 2 litre via nasal cannula and his urine in bag is very cloudy and dark and strong smell we send urine for mcs and his skin we check and he has new redness on left heel we apply mepilex and put on turning chart two hourly and his pressure area on sacrum which was stage one last week is now stage two and we apply allevyn and photograph and tissue viability nurse informed by email and dietishin also inform because his wate is 61 kilo down from 65 three week ago and he eating very poorly only taking fortisip supplement and even that he sometimes throw away and his ngt was suggested by doctar but family refuse so we continue encourage oral intake and his blood glucose this morning was 16.8 and we give correctional insulin 4 unit as per sliding scale and recheck at 10am and it was 14.2 still high and doctar increase background insulin lantus from 14 to 18 unit and we document in kardex and his bowel not open in five day we give movicol two sachet and glycerin suppository and result pending and his canula in right forearm has redness and hard lump we remove it and doctar put new one in left anticubital fossa and iv fluids running normal saline 1 litre over 8 hour as he is not drinking enough and his allergy to penicillin is on front of kardex all staff aware and he has appointment for renal outpatient on 15th april but given current condition we may need to cancel and nursing home manager informed of overall deterioration and risk assessment updated and incident form done for repeated catheter self removal and it is the fifth incident this month alone family are being very helpful and cooperative and son say he will stay overnight tonight to help and we allow this and extra chair put in room for him

saara keli in room 207 okay she is the patient sarah not the nurse okay she had a very bad night she was vomitting again again since dinner time around 6pm and she vomit seven times total we record every time and she is now very dehydrate and weak and she complain of severe abdominal pain nine out of ten and she is doubled over and cannot straight herself and doctar on call came and examine and say acute abdomen query appendicitis or bowel obstruction and surgical team called and they came and assess and they decide she need ct scan urgent and porter come and take her at 1am for ct and result show small bowel obstruction partial and surgical team say conservative management for now nil by mouth strict and nasogastric tube inserted by doctar size 14 french and free drainage in place and output so far 400ml dark green bilious and we record and she has iv access two canula in situ one in each forearm and iv fluid running hartmanns 125ml per hour and she is also getting iv ondansetron 4mg every 6 hour for nausea and iv paracetamol 1gram every 6 hour for pain and iv buscopan 20mg as needed and her bp was 90 over 60 when she first become unwell we give 500ml iv bolus normal saline and bp come up to 108 over 72 and we monitor every 30 minute and her heart rate is 118 tachycardia and temp is 38.9 high and bloods sent for full blood count urea electrolyte creatinine lactate blood culture two set and we are awaiting result and family notified husband came he is very anxious and we explain everything and doctar speak to him and she is nil by mouth strict and all oral medication held and she is on strict fluid balance and urine output via catheter 25ml per hour which is adequate and she is for repeat review by surgical team at 8am and if no improvement she going to theatre for possible surgery and we have consent form ready and she sign herself she is competent and she understand the risk and her next of kin detail is on file and allergy nil known

dayved keen rom 414 okay first i must say the room number on the board say 414 but the room door say 414b so i am not sure which one is correct please clarify also david is very unwell today his breathing is very very bad and he is using accessory muscle and he is hunching over and his sats drop to 88 percent on 4 litre oxygen and we increase to 15 litre via nonrebreather mask and sats come up to 93 percent and doctar emergency bleep and they came immediately and examine and say he has acute exacerbation of copd and they start him on back to back nebuliser salbutamol 2.5mg and ipratropium 500mcg and iv hydrocortisone 200mg and iv magnesium sulphate 2gram over 20 minute and they contact itu and itu consultant came and assess and they say he is for high dependency monitoring but not itu level yet and we transfer him to hdu room with one to one nursing and his arterial blood gas taken and result show ph 7.28 pco2 68 po2 58 bicarbonate 30 very concerning and doctar say if not improving in one hour they will need to intubate and family called daughter aoife she is on her way from galway and we contact her mobile and she is very upset and doctar will speak to her when she arrive and his news2 score is 9 which is very high and we escalate as per protocol and crash trolley outside room and defib ready and we document everything in nursing note and his weight is 47 kilo very underweight and he is on nutritional support 500ml ensure plus three time daily but intake poor and dietishin alerted and his background medications include spiriva once daily fostair two puff twice daily and theophylline 200mg twice daily all held during acute episode and doctar will review and restart when stabilise and he has advance care planning document in file do not resuscitate discussed previously with patient and family and this is documented and dated and signed by consultant

jozyfen flanagan room 118 she is very difficult to assess today because she is not cooperative and she keep saying leave me alone leave me alone and she push staff away when we try do obs and she hit staff nurse on duty today on the arm when we try take her blood pressure we complete incident form and this is the third time she physical aggressive this week alone previous two incident are documented and behavioural support team referral already sent last week still waiting response and today doctar came and assess mental capacity and she has capacity to refuse so we cannot force and we document this clearly and her bp last successful reading two day ago was 165 over 95 and we note she look more flushed today and we suspect bp may be high and we contact gp to discuss and gp say to try and persude her gently and if she refuse we document and escalate to consultant and she also refusing food and drink since this morning and she only take small sip water when staff she like come and offer so we identify which staff she respond best to and we put this in care plan that only those staff approach her for care and her catheter which was insert last week is now possibly blocked as urine output very low since midday only 50ml in 6 hour and we try flush it and 100ml came out and then stop again and doctar aware and considering whether to remove and try pad instead as she is fighting the catheter and pulling at it and her skin is intact no pressur area but she is very thin bmi 16.2 and we monitor closely and family son joseph he is aware and he say she can be like this sometimes and it pass but this time seem worse and we recommend family meeting and he agree and we schedule for next thursday 2pm and social worker invite also and her medication is being put in yogurt to help her take it and she sometimes take and sometime not and we record every time and the risperidone 0.5mg twice daily which was started last month for agitation she has not been taking reliably and doctar aware

meri oneal room 309 she woke up at 4am complaining very bad headache she say it is the worst headache of her life she never have headache like this before and she is very very distress and we take her seriously because this is new onset sudden severe headache and we bleep doctar immediately and doctar come and examine within 5 minute and they are very concern also and they call ambulance immediately for transfer to hospital query subarachnoid haemorrhage and we start monitoring obs every 15 minute and her bp is 210 over 130 very dangerously high and gcs is 14 she is slightly confusion and her left pupil is slightly larger than right pupil and doctar document this carefully and she is vomiting also and photophobic she is covering her eye from the light and phonophobic covering her ear and we give her nothing by mouth in case she need surgery and we make her comfortable in darkened room and we hold all her regular medication and we prepare transfer letter with all her medication list and allergy which is penicillin and sulphonamide and her next of kin is daughter siobhan and we call her and she is coming immediately from dublin and she is very very upset and doctar speak to her on phone to explain and ambulance arrive in 18 minute and paramedic assess and agree with transfer decision and she is taken at 4.52am and we call receiving hospital to give verbal handover and the nurse on duty in the hospital is aware and doctar there is also alerted and we document everything with time stamp and complete safeguarding check as this is sudden neurological event and the ward manager is informed by phone even though it is nighttime because of severity of this incident

brigit keli at room 225 okay so bridget she had another fall this is now her sixth fall this month sixth time we are doing everything possible and we have bed sensor and floor mat and chair sensor and low bed and she still falling and we done root cause analysis after fourth fall and we implement all the recommendation but she still fall and this morning she fall in bathroom she was trying to get to toilet herself without pressing call bell and she fall and hit her head on the basin and there is laceration on her forehead 3cm long and we apply pressure and it stop bleeding but the wound is gaping and doctar came and they put two suture in and apply dressing and head injury observation chart in place and we do neuro obs every 30 minute for 4 hour and all obs normal so far and she also has bruise on her right shoulder and her right shoulder movement is restrict and she is complaining pain 7 out of 10 and xray done this morning and result show no fracture just soft tissue injury and we give cocodamol 2 tablet and pain settle to 4 and she has a history of benign positional vertigo which may be contributing to falls and vestibular physiotherapy referral done last week and first appointment is next wednesday and family are very very unhappy they say we are not watching her close enough and daughter patricia she is threatening to make formal complaint and we explain all the measure in place and ward manager spoke with family and we offer safeguarding team involvement and formal review meeting and family agree to meeting on friday and she is now on one to one supervision until risk assessment review and her medication review done today and doctar stop her zopiclone sleeping tablet as this is likely contributing and stop her mirtazapine which can cause dizziness and the doctar say benefit risk reviewed and we document and she is for physiotherapy review for gait and balance tomorrow and occupational therapy for bathroom equipment assessment also and incident form done and datix completed and notification to hiqa also because this is the sixth fall

jonn morphy room 401 he is end stage renal failure on palliative pathway and he is very deteriorate today his urine output in last 24 hour is only 80ml total and he is anuric now last 4 hour zero urine and his creatinine last result on monday was 890 and urea 42 and potassium was 6.8 very dangerously high and doctar decide not to repeat blood today as he is on comfort care only and his family are aware that he is in final stage and his wife bridie and his two son donal and colm are at bedside and they have been here since yesterday morning and we provide tea and coffee and allow them stay outside visiting hour and chaplain father declan came this morning and did anointing of the sick and family are taking comfort and he is on syringe driver morphine 15mg plus midazolam 10mg plus hyoscine 1.2mg all over 24 hour and pain score is 1 out of 10 family say he look comfortable and we do mouth care every hour with pink swabs and lips moisturise with yellow gel and his breathing is cheyne stokes pattern since last night with long pause up to 25 second between breath and we explain this to family and reassure them this is normal at this stage and his extremities are mottled and cold and his colour is grey and family ask how long more and we say we cannot say exactly but signs suggest very soon and we call doctar and they came and assess and they are in agreement and they speak beautifully to family and his blood pressure last taken yesterday evening was 70 over 40 and we decide not to continue taking bp as it is distressing and not changing management and we document this decision and his nutrition is nil all family understand and support this and no active treatment in place and all his previous medication is discontinued and only comfort medication via syringe driver continuing and we check syringe driver every hour and document and the next syringe driver renewal is due at 14.00 today and we have prescription ready and medication drawn up and his pressure area on sacrum is being care for very gently with soft silicone dressing and position change very gently with pillow support every 2 hour so he is not in discomfort and we turn log roll style with family helping if they want and family say they are so grateful for the care and the dignity being shown

katilin nolan rom 316 she is type one diabetic on insulin pump and this morning the pump alarm and we call the diabetes specialist nurse hotline and they advise to check the cannula site of the pump and the site was very red and induration and the cannula was block and we remove the pump cannula and insert new cannula on left abdominal wall as per her instruction card and restart pump and alarm stop and her bsl before all this was 22.4 very high and we give correctional dose of humalog 6 unit subcutaneous as per her sick day rule card and recheck one hour later and it is 18.6 still high and we give further 4 unit and recheck and it is 15.2 improving and diabetes specialist nurse coming this afternoon to review and also she has ketone strip and we do ketone check and it was 2.8 moderate ketone and we push fluid and she is drinking well 250ml water every hour and we do hourly bsl and ketone check and she is also complaining of abdominal pain which she say can happen when blood sugar is high and she look pale and nauseous and we give antiemetic ondansetron 4mg sublingual and she is not vomiting yet but feel like she might and doctar aware and they come to review and they say if ketone go above 3 or if she vomit then we will need to start dka protocol and transfer to hospital and we explain this to her and she understand and she is anxious and we reassure and her mother is with her who is very knowledgeable about diabetes management and she is helping monitor and the mother is asking many question and we try answer all and doctar is very patient and explain everything to both of them and we document all communication and her latest bsl is 12.6 and ketone is 1.8 both improving so dka protocol not started and we continue close monitoring

soozan obreyen room in ward b not sure exact number maybe 222 or 223 they look same she confuse us also she have chest infection very bad on day one iv antibiotic meropenem because her previous culture grow pseudomonas resistant to most antibiotic and she has history of bronchiectasis and multiple admission with same bacteria and she on nebuliser four time daily and chest physiotherapy twice daily and her sputum is very thick and green and large amount and she is exhausted from coughing all night and she barely sleep and she is on high flow oxygen 10 litre face mask and sats still only 93 percent and rr is 28 very fast and she is using neck muscle to breathe and her news2 score is 11 and we escalate already and itu team came and assess and they say she is on the boundary and they will review again in 2 hour and she is not for intubation as per her advance care plan but she has agreed to bipap and respiratory team came and fit bipap mask and she is now on bipap ipap 16 epap 6 and sats improve to 96 percent on bipap and she is more comfortable and doctar say this is good sign and we monitor every 15 minute and her blood culture taken and we await result and her previous bloods from yesterday crp was 345 very high and white cell 24 and albumin 18 low and she is malnourished bmi 15.8 and dietishin has been involve and she is on nasogastric feed ensure plus 80ml per hour and ng tube position confirmed by ph strip before feed start and she is also on thromboprophylaxis enoxaparin 40mg subcutaneous daily and her allergy is documented cephalosporin cause anaphylaxis and this is on front of all chart and wristband and she has chronic kidney disease stage 3 and meropenem dose is adjust for renal function as per microbiology advice and trough level to be taken tomorrow morning before the 3rd dose and we remind night staff to ensure this is done and she has family sister eileen who is her nok and she is aware of severity and she came this evening and spoke with doctar and she is very calm and asking appropriate question and she understand the situation is serious

jeral wals room 510 he is post total knee replacement day three and he is very very difficult patient he refuse everything he refuse physiotherapy he refuse occupational therapy he refuse to do his exercises he refuse to eat he refuse medication and he is verbally aggressive to all staff and using very bad language and he is throwing things he throw the water jug this morning and it break and he throw his meal tray at lunchtime and the food go all over floor and the healthcare assistant who clean it up is upset and we complete incident form and wellbeing referral for staff and he is shouting that nobody know what they doing and he demand to speak to consultant and we arrange and consultant came and spoke to him for 20 minute and he calm down little bit after and then he complain his knee is very very painful 10 out of 10 and he say the pain medication is not working and we review his chart and he is on regular paracetamol 1gram every 6 hour and regular ibuprofen 400mg three time daily with food and oramorph 10mg every 4 hour as prn and he say he is not getting the prn and we check the mar chart and we see the prn was not offered since yesterday morning and we escalate this to the ward manager and apologise to the patient and we give him oramorph 10mg now and document and review the prn prescribing practice and we also note his knee is very swollen and hot and red and doctar review and they are concern about infection and they aspirate the knee joint and send sample for mcs and also send blood for crp esr and white cell and crp come back 187 which is high and white cell 14.5 and his temperature is 37.9 low grade and they say query surgical site infection and they start him on iv flucloxacillin 2gram every 6 hour and orthopedic consultant informed and they came and review and they agree and they say if culture grow bacteria they may need to take him back to theatre for washout and we explain this to him and now he is frighten and he is much more cooperative and we explain we understand he is in a lot of pain and we are going to manage his pain better and we will review prn frequency and doctar increase oramorph to 20mg every 4 hour and add oxycodone 5mg twice daily regular and his family wife patricia came and she is able to calm him better than staff and we involve her in care and she will stay with him this evening and she is helpful and she say he is always like this when he is afraid and in pain and underneath he is actually a lovely man
